description: Fix regressions in layout edit mode (X button and save button unresponsive), shop panel image sizing/alignment, and pet rendering/dragging issues

userStories.title: Fix X remove button clicks in layout edit mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click the X button on furniture during edit mode so that I can send items back to inventory.
acceptanceCriteria: Investigate input handling in _handle_edit_input() (scripts/main.gd:996) — the function calls get_viewport().set_input_as_handled() at line 1012 for all left clicks; verify that remove button clicks return early BEFORE this call and that the event actually reaches the Button's GUI handler

Verify _is_click_on_remove_button() (scripts/main.gd:966) rect calculation matches the actual Button global rect — it manually computes Rect2(fnode.global_position + btn.position, btn.size) which may not match the Button's true screen position when parented to a Node2D
Remove buttons respond to clicks on all furniture items during edit mode
Clicking X removes the furniture from the scene and adds it to inventory
Stacked furniture on top of removed furniture is also returned to inventory
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Fix save button click in layout edit mode
userStories.description: As a user, I want to click the Save button in the slide menu to save my layout and exit edit mode instead of pressing ESC.
acceptanceCriteria: Investigate why Save Edit button (scripts/slide_menu.gd:151) does not respond during edit mode — likely same root cause as US-001 input consumption, or the slide_menu._unhandled_input auto-close (scripts/slide_menu.gd:172) closing the menu before the button click registers
Clicking Save Edit button in the slide menu exits edit mode and saves the layout
Button label reverts to Edit Layout after saving
Furniture positions are persisted to save_data.json
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Fix shop panel image sizing and alignment
userStories.description: As a user, I want shop item images to fit within their grid cell buttons so the shop looks correct.
acceptanceCriteria: Fix TextureRect in _create_grid_cell() (scripts/shop.gd:155-161) — EXPAND_FIT_WIDTH_PROPORTIONAL (line 159) and PRESET_CENTER anchors (line 161) cause overflow when source textures are larger than the 48x48 target; use EXPAND_IGNORE_SIZE or EXPAND_FIT_WIDTH with clip_contents on the parent button
Item images fit within their button bounds (64x64 cell, 48x48 icon target)
Images are centered within their buttons with no overflow or clipping artifacts
Layout remains correct across different numbers of items and varying grid column counts
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Fix pet rendering — in front of furniture, behind menus
userStories.description: As a user, I want the pet to appear in front of furniture but behind UI menus so it looks natural and menus remain usable.
acceptanceCriteria: Pet renders visually in front of all furniture sprites
Pet renders behind slide menu, shop panel, inventory panel, settings panel, and pet selection panel

The current z_index approach (scripts/main.gd:113, pet z_index=1 vs furniture container z_index=0) may need adjustment — z_index only works within the same parent; UI Control nodes render on top of Node2D regardless, so verify the actual rendering order and fix if menus appear behind the pet
Menus and panels remain fully clickable and visually on top of the pet
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Fix pet dragging when overlapping furniture
userStories.description: As a user, I want to grab and drag the pet even when it overlaps furniture so it does not feel stuck.
acceptanceCriteria: Investigate pet _is_point_on_pet() (scripts/pet.gd:673) and _input() (scripts/pet.gd:640) — pet click detection may be blocked by the passthrough polygon excluding the pet area when it overlaps furniture, or by furniture nodes consuming input first
Pet can be grabbed by clicking on it even when overlapping furniture
Dragging the pet works smoothly regardless of what is behind it
Releasing the pet after drag triggers FALLING state correctly
Pet cannot be grabbed when clicking on a menu or panel area — menus take priority
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
